name: Lamb Loin in Puff Pastry
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Veal Stock or Chicken Broth
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Lamb Loin In Puff Pastry:
- 17 1/4 oz Package Puff Pastry Sheets
- 24 oz Boneless Lamb Loin Chops
- Hot Cooked Vegetables
- Red Wine Sauce:
- In a large saucepan, brown shallots, mushrooms, and ground lamb in butter. Stir in red wine, chicken stock, and veal stock. Bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat and add juniper berries, bay leaf, thyme, garlic, and vinegar. Simmer for 10 minutes.
- Strain the sauce through a fine mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding solids.
- Return strained sauce to the pan and stir in lemon juice, salt, and pepper to taste. Keep warm.
- Nut Stuffing: Pulse pistachio nuts until finely chopped. Add chestnut puree, ground lamb, and brandy to the food processor; pulse briefly until blended.
Assembly:
1. Let puff pastry come to room temperature as directed on the package.
2. Spoon about 1/4 of the stuffing onto each lamb loin.
3. Mold the stuffing around the lamb loin using your hands until completely enclosed.
4. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
5. Gently unfold one sheet of puff pastry on a lightly floured surface.
6. Press the seams together at both ends to seal.
7. Cut the pastry into two rectangles, each measuring approximately 10x8 inches.
8. Place a wrapped lamb loin crosswise in the center of each pastry rectangle.
9. Fold the long sides of the pastry over the meat and fold again to seal.
10. Transfer each pastry-lined tray to the preheated oven and bake until golden brown.
